narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with significant firepower anchored by Yordan Alvarez, whose 112.26 projection is fueled by a stellar matchup against Brayan Bello and a distinct BvP edge. Kyle Schwarber and Pete Crow-Armstrong also provide high-upside stability, benefiting from favorable lineup positioning and elite wOBA profiles that should drive counting stats across the board. Colt Keith remains a critical pivot point; his projected #3 spot combined with a platoon advantage against Pfaadt makes him a prime candidate to maximize Roto efficiency in the heart of the order.

The remainder of the lineup presents a series of calculated risks as we navigate tougher matchups, most notably Junior Caminero facing a difficult encounter with Brandon Woodruff, where his production is severely dampened by the SP skill disadvantage. Christian Yelich faces a challenging left-on-left platoon scenario against Shane McClanahan, while the catcher tandem of Dillon Dingler and Logan O’Hoppe are positioned toward the bottom of the order, limiting their scoring potential. We will monitor lineup confirmation closely, as any shift from these assumed slots—specifically for the fringe plays—could necessitate a last-minute adjustment to preserve our efficiency metrics.
